batches.title: Hard-Boiled Eggs (for Wed & Thu breakfasts)
steps: Fill a large saucepan with cold water and bring to a rolling boil.
Gently lower 10 eggs into the water using a spoon to avoid cracking.
Boil for exactly 9 minutes for firm yolks.
Transfer immediately to a bowl of ice-cold water and leave for 3 minutes.
Peel all eggs carefully under running water. Pat dry.
Place peeled eggs in an airtight container with a damp paper towel to keep them moist.
Refrigerate. Label container: 'Boiled eggs — use by Thursday morning (day 4).'
yields: 10 peeled hard-boiled eggs (5 per person across Wed & Thu breakfasts)

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Breakfast — Kefir Smoothie & Boiled Eggs on Sourdough: take 2 eggs per person from container, halve, season with salt and pepper, place on toasted sourdough. Blend kefir smoothie fresh.
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Breakfast — Kefir Smoothie & Boiled Eggs on Sourdough: retrieve last 2 eggs per person (day 4 — final safe day), halve, plate on toasted sourdough. Blend smoothie fresh with banana and strawberries.

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Roasted Bell Peppers (for Mon lunch & Wed dinner)
steps: Slice 700 g mixed red and yellow bell peppers into strips about 1.5 cm wide. Discard seeds and white pith.
Spread pepper strips in a single layer on a large lined baking tray.
Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, sprinkle with dried oregano, smoked paprika, black pepper and a pinch of sea salt. Toss to coat evenly.
Roast for 25–28 minutes, turning once halfway, until peppers are soft and lightly caramelised at the edges.
Remove from oven, allow to cool completely before refrigerating.
Transfer to an airtight container. Label: 'Roasted peppers — use by Thursday (day 4).'
yields: ~600 g roasted bell peppers (for Mon lunch 250 g + Wed dinner 180 g)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mexican Beef & Lentil Bowl: reheat 150 g (Diego) / 100 g (Diana) roasted peppers in pan 2 min alongside reheated ground beef, layer over spinach and lentils in bowl with tortillas.
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Tilapia Tacos: reheat 100 g (Diego) / 80 g (Diana) roasted peppers briefly in pan with a pinch of smoked paprika, layer into warm corn tortillas with flaked tilapia, fresh spinach and mustard-lime sauce.

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.
